rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks only into implementation scopes when binding implementation-level data flows, preserving AADL component structure.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL scope boundaries: use implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings, not type-level contracts.
Only reference features and connections explicitly declared in the AADL model within AGREE annexes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes in implementations must use valid subcomponent ports or declared variables, not abstract component paths.
Do not encode AADL properties or event ports as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as typed data ports.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing guarantee statements or type-level contracts in implementation annexes.
Do not assume AADL property names (e.g., Period, Deadline) are visible as variables in AGREE.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
